Title: A gossypol biosynthetic intermediate disturbs plant defence response
Journal: Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society B: Biological Sciences

Common_Genes.Gene_Name: GRXS3; GRXS4; GRXS5; GRXS7; GRXS8; ROXY10
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: C234 treatment strongly represses these glutaredoxin genes during pathogen challenge, accompanying distorted defence responses and increased susceptibility
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Downregulated in leaves after C234, Psm, and especially Psm + C234 treatment
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves under C234 and pathogen treatment
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: C234 treatment; Pseudomonas syringae pv. maculicola ES4326 infection
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A cluster of six glutaredoxin genes was significantly repressed by C234-associated treatments. 2. Five clustered genes (AtGRXS3/4/5/7/8) were among the most strongly downregulated genes after C234 treatment. 3. Their repression is associated with altered redox-related responses during C234-disturbed defence.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: These genes are responsive markers/affected genes rather than primary engineered causal genes in the gossypol pathway; included because they are specifically highlighted in Results as metabolism/defence-related core responsive genes.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PR1 was induced by C234/Psm-associated defence responses and used as an upregulation reference
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: C234 treatment; Pseudomonas syringae pv. maculicola infection
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PR1 is among defence-related genes responsive to C234 and Psm. 2. It showed stronger response in Psm + C234 plants. 3. It served as a defence marker rather than a causal metabolic gene.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Marker gene, not a primary subject of mechanistic experimentation.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: sesquiterpene phytoalexin branch of cotton terpenoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required to prevent accumulation of a toxic pathway intermediate during gossypol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for detoxifying a phytotoxic intermediate; highly active enzyme in pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing CYP71BE79 causes accumulation of C234 and enhanced susceptibility of cotton to Rhizoctonia solani; active CYP71BE79 prevents C234 accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Gossypol and related sesquiterpenes are major cotton phytoalexins involved in disease defence
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene silencing; metabolite profiling by GC-MS and LC-MS; pathogen infection assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71BE79 normally metabolizes the gossypol intermediate C234. 2. Silencing CYP71BE79 leads to C234 accumulation, especially in hypocotyl and root. 3. C234 accumulation is associated with reduced disease resistance, indicating CYP71BE79 protects cotton from a phytotoxic intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintain or enhance CYP71BE79 activity in cotton breeding or metabolic engineering to avoid toxic C234 accumulation while preserving gossypol-based defence
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing causes accumulation of a phytotoxic intermediate and reduced disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper focuses on biological activity of the intermediate C234 rather than direct engineering of gossypol content; authors note CYP71BE79 has more than tenfold higher maximum activity than other identified gossypol-pathway enzymes.
